subject: Human Resources
title: Workplace Safety Policies
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Workplace Safety Policies.

hook.text: A factory worker is injured. There's no safety protocol, no training, and no incident report process.

buildup.text: Workplace safety policies outline hazard prevention, emergency procedures, and incident reporting requirements.

discovery.text: In the U.S., OSHA requires employers to provide a safe workplace—violations bring steep fines and liability.

twist.text: Safety isn't just for factories. Office ergonomics, mental health support, and fire drills all count.

climax.text: Companies with strong safety cultures see fewer injuries, lower insurance costs, and higher employee trust.

punchline.text: Safety policies save lives and lawsuits.

quiz.question: What does OSHA require of employers?
options: To provide a safe workplace free of known hazards
